title: Lindsaea moorei
taxonomy: Lindsaea moorei is classified as a species within the genus Lindsaea, which is a part of the family Lindsaeaceae. This family belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into the subgroup Eupolypods I, which is a subgroup of the larger group Polypodiophyta. This group is a subclass of the division Pteridophyta, also known as ferns. The division Pteridophyta is a class of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.
uses: Lindsaea moorei has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. intro: Lindsaea moorei, also known as the 'New Caledonian tree fern', is a species of plant in the genus Lindsaea, which belongs to the family Lindsaeaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
